meaning: adj.未蒙损失的,未受伤害的
examples: They escaped from the crash uninjured.
The man's wife, a passenger in the van, was uninjured in the accident.
Luckily, the patient was uninjured, but we saw a chance to help.
Rescuers said the boy, who was visiting the area with his family, was uninjured.
The driver of the lorry, a 35-year-old man from Toftwood near Dereham, was uninjured.
